text:
Paris 2024 Olympic organisers have been hit by a major embarrassment as the men’s triathlon has been postponed due to bacteria in the Seine River despite a controversial $2.4 billion clean-up.

The Paris 2024 Olympic Games have officially postponed the men’s triathlon due to elevated levels of water contamination in the Seine River.

The decision was made at 4.15am local time on Tuesday, less than four hours ahead of the scheduled start time of 8am (4pm AEST).

While organisers were confident the water quality would improve by the morning, rigorous bacteriological analyses confirmed unsafe levels of enterococci and E. coli in the water.

An official statement declared: “Following a meeting on water quality held on 30 July at 3.30am attended by Paris 2024… decision has been made to postpone the men’s triathlon event.”

“The tests carried out in the Seine today revealed water quality levels that did not provide sufficient guarantees to allow the event to be held.

“We will provide the athletes who are due to take part in the men’s triathlon event with all information on its postponement, as well as real-time updates on the situation.”

The triathlon has now been delayed by a day.

pic.twitter.com/4nlBRz2ibk

The rescheduling has come as a blow to both athletes and organisers, who have been grappling with the unpredictable water quality issues in recent days.

France had reportedly spent almost $2.5 billion on cleaning the Seine for the Olympics, causing Parisiennes tothreaten a poo protestfor perceived excessive expenses.

While the river was thought to be adequately clean, recent heavy rains have significantly impacted water quality due to increased currents and raw sewage.

The final testing on Tuesday confirmed the levels of harmful bacteria exceeded safety thresholds.

Under the contingency plan, if swimming has been deemed unsafe on the rescheduled date on Wednesday, the event will have to be converted into a duathlon.

However, with the postponement, organisers are hopeful the water quality could improve sufficiently by Wednesday morning.

This postponement marks the first time in Olympic triathlon history that a full triathlon has been rescheduled due to water contamination.

Organisers hope to avoid a nightmare scenario like the 2023 World Cup in Sunderland, UK when collective gastroenteritis struck the event and more than 57 participants fell ill.

The Paris 2024 organising committee has assured that all necessary measures are being taken to address the contamination issues and prevent future disruptions.

Several athletes have reacted to the news on social media and most appear to have taken to challenge in their stride.

Tokyo 2020 champion Kristian Blummenfelt posted, “No racing due to water quality” while Paris 2024 favourite Alex Yee wrote “We race tomorrow”.

Australians Matthew Hauser and Luke Willian are both set to compete for gold in the triathlon now scheduled to commence at 6.45pm on Wednesday, August 1 (AEST).

title: Paris 2024 postpones triathlon due to water contamination setback as Seine River deemed unsuitable for swimming | Sky News Australia
description: The Paris 2024 Olympic Games have officially postponed the men’s triathlon due to elevated levels of water contamination in the Seine River.
